message:
fix(fcp-oauth): repair two broken redaction tests committed today

REVIEW-SWEEP fresh-eyes pass on today's redaction commits found two
unit tests that were committed broken — they fail on `cargo test -p
fcp-oauth --lib` against current main.

1. `oauth1::tests::test_request_token_debug` (introduced by e3474510
   "redact oauth debug secrets"). Same broken-single-letter-sentinel
   pattern that 3013e3cf (br-8i1x4) explicitly fixed in oauth2's
   `test_callback_debug`: the test set `token = "t".to_string()` then
   asserted `!debug.contains("t")`, but the Debug output literally
   says `RequestToken { token: "[REDACTED]", ... }` which is dense
   with the letter `t` (RequestToken, token, token_secret). The
   assertion is unsatisfiable with the redacted Debug impl. Rewritten
   to use distinctive multi-character sentinel values for both
   `token` and `token_secret`, with separate `!contains(<sentinel>)`
   assertions per field. Comment names the prior failure mode so a
   future refactor cannot regress to the single-letter shape.

2. `error::tests::state_mismatch_empty_strings` (orphaned by
   8c60d086 "redact state values and upstream response bodies from
   OAuth error Display"). The Display surface for StateMismatch was
   reduced to the constant string `"OAuth state mismatch"` so an
   attacker who triggers the error cannot leak the expected CSRF /
   anti-replay state. The test still asserted `display.contains("expected")`
   and `display.contains("got")` against the pre-redaction output.
   Rewritten to assert the new redacted constant and document why
   empty-string inputs are still expected to produce that exact
   constant (no truncation, no panic, no byte-length oracle).

The companion `error::tests::state_mismatch_redacts_state_values`
already pins the non-empty path; this test now pins the empty-string
path.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-oauth --lib — 623/623 pass (was 621/623
on main pre-fix, with these two failures).

No production code change. No public API change. Test-only repair
that closes the silent-failure window the prior commits opened.
